req.title: Search patients (boundary)
req.statement: A clinician shall search patients by name, MRN, or DOB; only patients in their care shall be returned. Boundary cases on timestamps and numeric elements must be tested.

doc.reasoning: R-HLT-038 requires three orthogonal test dimensions: (1) correct filtering of patients by name/MRN/DOB for a clinician's own patients (EP positive), (2) access isolation ensuring patients outside the clinician's care are not returned (DT/NEG), (3) boundary values on DOB timestamps such as midnight UTC edge and max-date semantics (BVA). A fourth test covers the 400 error path for malformed queries (NEG). Each test uses a shared `clinician_auth_headers` fixture and a `fhir_base_url` fixture, plus a small `<assert_audit_log>` helper placeholder to verify access logging without inflating line counts.

tests.name: test_search_patient_by_mrn_returns_own_patient
preconditions: Patient with MRN='MRN-00042' is assigned to this clinician
Patient with MRN='MRN-00099' is NOT assigned to this clinician
actions: GET /Patient?search with query param identifier=MRN-00042 using clinician auth headers
Capture HTTP status and response body
Verify audit log entry is created
expected: Response is a FHIR Bundle with resourceType='Bundle'
Bundle contains exactly one entry matching MRN-00042
Audit log records the search event for this clinician
trace: R-HLT-038 / positive path: search by MRN
R-HLT-038 / expected outcome: filtered bundle returned
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_search_patient_by_mrn_returns_own_patient(fhir_base_url, clinician_auth_headers):
    mrn = "MRN-00042"
    resp = requests.get(
        f"{fhir_base_url}/Patient",
        params={"search": "", "identifier": mrn},
        headers=clinician_auth_headers,
        timeout=10,
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 200, f"Expected 200, got {resp.status_code}"
    body = resp.json()
    assert body.get("resourceType") == "Bundle"
    entries = body.get("entry", [])
    assert len(entries) == 1, f"Expected 1 entry, got {len(entries)}"
    identifiers = entries[0]["resource"].get("identifier", [])
    mrn_values = [i["value"] for i in identifiers if i.get("value")]
    assert mrn in mrn_values, f"MRN {mrn} not found in {mrn_values}"
    <assert_audit_log>(clinician_auth_headers, event="patient-search", mrn=mrn)

tests.name: test_search_patient_outside_care_not_returned
preconditions: Patient with MRN='MRN-00099' exists in the system but is NOT in this clinician's care
Clinician has zero patients sharing the name 'Outside Patient'
actions: GET /Patient?search with query param family=Outside using clinician auth headers
Capture HTTP status and response body
expected: Bundle entry list is empty (total=0 or entry absent)
Patient MRN-00099 is not present in any entry
trace: R-HLT-038 / access control: only patients in clinician's care returned
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_search_patient_outside_care_not_returned(fhir_base_url, clinician_auth_headers):
    resp = requests.get(
        f"{fhir_base_url}/Patient",
        params={"search": "", "family": "Outside"},
        headers=clinician_auth_headers,
        timeout=10,
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 200, f"Expected 200, got {resp.status_code}"
    body = resp.json()
    assert body.get("resourceType") == "Bundle"
    entries = body.get("entry", [])
    assert entries == [], f"Expected empty bundle, got {len(entries)} entries"
    total = body.get("total", 0)
    assert total == 0, f"Expected total=0, got {total}"

tests.name: test_search_patient_by_dob_midnight_utc_boundary
preconditions: Patient 'P-BOUNDARY' has DOB='2000-01-01' (midnight UTC boundary) and is in clinician's care
No other patient in clinician's care shares this DOB
actions: GET /Patient?search with query param birthdate=2000-01-01 using clinician auth headers
expected: Response for 2000-01-01: HTTP 200, Bundle contains exactly P-BOUNDARY
Response for 1999-12-31: HTTP 200, Bundle entry list is empty
trace: R-HLT-038 / boundary cases on timestamps must be tested
R-HLT-038 / BVA: exact DOB match at day boundary and adjacent day
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_search_patient_by_dob_midnight_utc_boundary(fhir_base_url, clinician_auth_headers):
    base = fhir_base_url
    hdrs = clinician_auth_headers

    resp_on = requests.get(
        f"{base}/Patient",
        params={"search": "", "birthdate": "2000-01-01"},
        headers=hdrs, timeout=10,
    )
    assert resp_on.status_code == 200
    body_on = resp_on.json()
    assert body_on.get("resourceType") == "Bundle"
    entries_on = body_on.get("entry", [])
    assert len(entries_on) == 1, f"Expected 1 entry for DOB 2000-01-01, got {len(entries_on)}"
    assert entries_on[0]["resource"].get("id") == "P-BOUNDARY"

    resp_before = requests.get(
        f"{base}/Patient",
        params={"search": "", "birthdate": "1999-12-31"},
        headers=hdrs, timeout=10,
    )
    assert resp_before.status_code == 200
    body_before = resp_before.json()
    assert body_before.get("resourceType") == "Bundle"
    assert body_before.get("entry", []) == [], "Expected empty bundle for adjacent day"

tests.name: test_search_patient_malformed_query_returns_400
actions: GET /Patient?search with query param birthdate=not-a-date (malformed date string)
Capture HTTP status and response body
expected: Response body contains a FHIR OperationOutcome with severity='error'
No patient data is leaked in the error response
trace: R-HLT-038 / error pathway: 400 when query malformed
R-HLT-038 / NEG: invalid parameter format rejected
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_search_patient_malformed_query_returns_400(fhir_base_url, clinician_auth_headers):
    resp = requests.get(
        f"{fhir_base_url}/Patient",
        params={"search": "", "birthdate": "not-a-date"},
        headers=clinician_auth_headers,
        timeout=10,
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 400, f"Expected 400, got {resp.status_code}"
    body = resp.json()
    resource_type = body.get("resourceType")
    assert resource_type == "OperationOutcome", (
        f"Expected OperationOutcome, got {resource_type}"
    )
    issues = body.get("issue", [])
    assert len(issues) > 0, "OperationOutcome must contain at least one issue"
    severities = [i.get("severity") for i in issues]
    assert "error" in severities or "fatal" in severities, (
        f"Expected error severity, got {severities}"
    )
    assert "entry" not in body, "Patient data must not appear in error response"

tests.name: test_search_patient_by_name_returns_filtered_bundle
preconditions: Patient 'Jane Doe' with ID 'P-JANE' is in clinician's care
No other patient named 'Jane Doe' is in this clinician's care
actions: GET /Patient?search with query params family=Doe&given=Jane using clinician auth headers
Capture HTTP status, bundle, and audit log
expected: Bundle contains exactly one entry with id='P-JANE'
Audit log records the search event
trace: R-HLT-038 / positive path: search by name
R-HLT-038 / expected outcome: filtered bundle returned
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_search_patient_by_name_returns_filtered_bundle(fhir_base_url, clinician_auth_headers):
    resp = requests.get(
        f"{fhir_base_url}/Patient",
        params={"search": "", "family": "Doe", "given": "Jane"},
        headers=clinician_auth_headers,
        timeout=10,
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 200, f"Expected 200, got {resp.status_code}"
    body = resp.json()
    assert body.get("resourceType") == "Bundle"
    entries = body.get("entry", [])
    assert len(entries) == 1, f"Expected 1 entry, got {len(entries)}"
    resource = entries[0].get("resource", {})
    assert resource.get("id") == "P-JANE", (
        f"Expected P-JANE, got {resource.get('id')}"
    )
    <assert_audit_log>(clinician_auth_headers, event="patient-search", patient_id="P-JANE")
